summary: Mature ecosystems use visible companion files when metadata must remain independent of the annotated payload.

items.text: The REUSE Specification permits `<filename>.license` companion files and identifies them as the option for files that cannot carry comments.
OpenPGP describes detached signatures as separate entities stored as standalone files alongside or independently of the signed data.
GNU Coreutils documents checksum output that records a digest with its file name and can later verify the file against that record.
Adobe documents XMP sidecar files for metadata that cannot be embedded in the media format.
TypeScript declaration files contain type information without implementation, and declaration layout mirrors the JavaScript library it describes.
